intro: I am applying for the Data Engineer role focused on AWS, Snowflake, PySpark, and SQL. My background aligns closely with building cloud data pipelines, data warehouse models, and reliable analytics-ready datasets.
body: At CITI, I built Python/Pandas ETL pipelines ingesting telemetry from more than 6,000 endpoints and transforming raw operational data into structured datasets for reporting, forecasting, and stakeholder decision-making. I also developed AWS-based data workflows using S3, Glue, and Redshift to support scalable data processing and analytics workloads.

My experience includes data warehousing, schema design, historical retention models, SQL-driven reporting, and pipeline troubleshooting across large-scale enterprise environments. I have worked with PySpark, Hadoop/Hive concepts, Parquet storage patterns, and Git-based development practices, and I am actively building fluency in Snowflake, dbt, Databricks, and modern lakehouse patterns.

I bring a strong mix of hands-on data engineering, cloud platform experience, and collaboration with technical and business stakeholders. I am comfortable participating in Agile delivery, translating requirements into data solutions, and supporting dependable ETL pipelines that serve analysts, engineers, and decision-makers.
conclusion: I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my AWS, Python, SQL, PySpark, and data warehousing experience to your data engineering team.
